reviews.review_title: Actual game is not the trailer DONT BUY
reviews.review_description: I bought the full version and immediately had to get a refund. The trailer is insanely inaccurate to the game visually and with its movements. The mechanics are crap, the movements are crap, the entire concept is crap. It heavily weighs on larping and being unrealistically patient for a battle. Delayed reaction time for your character to mimic real-life weight but its more like having springs for arms. Floppy movements. Grainy and blurry visuals. Settings are crap too. And let's not mention its all kids in this game. Its worth 7 bucks at best...this game could be great. Its just been massively skipped over in the finer details that make a game truly great.. sorry for the bad review. Just true

Writing a review on the demo version of this game since my friend let me bum the final version of the game off of them and play it, and wow, I was both impressed, awestruck and highly disappointed.

Battle Talent is a great game. The graphics look awesome, the combat itself actually feels very satisfying and like it doesn't pull any B.S. in the midst of a big fight (or at least not any B.S. that you can't work around and counter), and it's a really good execution from a really good concept.

However, after piling in at least 20+ hours on the main campaign and giving each individual additional mode a go, I can safely say that it's still not worth it, even after the kudos I gave. The game is extremely buggy. Whether it's an enemy somehow flinging you out of the map or into an area that gets you killed instantly or tank an unholy amount of damage, another player yoinking a weapon that you're holding from you or you getting hit from a mile away, the bugs are insane and even though the devs say they're working on it, it's very hard to believe that with how this game functions. The worst part of this game though (or the final one), arguably is the multiplayer. It is so unfathomably terrible. The game constantly lags, hit recognition is non-existent, the playerbase is somehow almost as obnoxious as Gorilla Tag's players and it's so disappointing. There's so many games to play and honestly, the demo of Battle Talent is a better bet than the should've been free lame final product.

Been playing since app lab; I've always loved this game. It's been my favorite since I started playing.


(TL;DR: There are SO many awesome things you can do in this game and you should totally buy it. )


The movement mechanics (once unlocked) are very fun, the combat is spectacular, and the vast weapon variety gives so much opportunity to the player to do new things & feel really cool.
There are also some REALLY cool weapons.
Additionally, there are arena fights, boss fights, and dungeon crawls, and a developing multiplayer mode!
Also, you can deflect melee attacks with your BARE HANDS.
The sandbox menu allows for lots of customization in various aspects, like unlimited air-jumps, lightweight weapons, invincibility, unlimited slow-motion, and so on.
There are some rather good mods to be found in the mod menu, too.
(Khopesherang, Wing of Astel, gesture magic, etc.)
Also, you can curb stomp goblins.
(Or people, if you want.)
There's more, but I think I've given enough information.

All this content in one game.

I thoroughly advise any and all vr combat game enjoyers to buy this game. It's a great time, and you should try it.
